name: Coconut Rockfish
recipeIngredient: 2 3/4 cups Freshly cut corn kernels OR 2 3/4 cups Frozen corn kernels
1 can Black ripe olives, sliced/pitted, drained
1 1/2 lbs Rockfish fillets, 1/2-inch thick, cut into 6 portions
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-4 quart pan over medium heat, stir shredded coconut until golden, 3-5 minutes. Remove from pan and set aside.
2. Add minced garlic, sliced green onions, and 1 tablespoon olive oil to the pan. Stir often over medium heat until onions are limp, 3-5 minutes.
3. Add corn, red bell pepper, black olives, and 2 tablespoons water; cook, covered, until corn is tender to bite, about 5 minutes. Mix in chopped parsley; keep relish warm.
4. Rinse rockfish fillets and pat dry. Rub fillets with lemon juice and remaining 1 1/2 tablespoons oil.
5. Arrange fillets in a single layer in a 12x17-inch broiler pan (without rack). Broil about 3 inches from heat for 3 minutes.
6. Turn fish over; broil until opaque, still moist-looking in the center of thickest part (test by cutting), 2-3 minutes longer.
7. Transfer fillets to a warm platter. Spoon corn relish onto platter and sprinkle shredded coconut over fish.
8. Garnish with lemon wedges and parsley sprigs. Season fish with lemon, salt, and pepper to taste.
